league.headline: The national show starts with the standings board and the daily pitching slate.
verified_notes: MLB.com standings provides the league board for division and wild-card framing.
MLB.com's probable-pitchers page provides the daily matchup slate.
ESPN's MLB transactions page provides the broad move tracker.
Current MLB standings snapshot includes Yankees 16-9, Red Sox 9-17, Angels 12-15, and Mariners 12-15.
Current probable-pitcher slate snapshot includes Padres vs D-backs, Athletics vs Rangers, Pirates vs Brewers, Angels vs Royals, and Yankees vs Astros.
stories: Use the standings board to identify the biggest truth of the day before going local.
Start with division leaders, ugly underperformers, and early wild-card pressure points.
Use the probable-pitcher slate to identify the best national watch items.
transactions: Use ESPN MLB transactions page for current verified league moves before air.
Do not add league-wide injury or roster chatter unless it is confirmed from the transaction log.

athletics.headline: The A's are in first, but the better question is whether the underlying signs make that start feel sturdy.
verified_notes: Snapshot verified from MLB.com team pages, ESPN transactions, and Baseball Savant.
Baseball Savant lists the Athletics at 14-12, first in the AL West, with a -8 run differential.
MLB.com's probable-pitchers page lists Jeffrey Springs and J.T. Ginn.
MLB.com transactions page shows Denzel Clarke to the 10-day injured list and Colby Thomas recalled on April 22.
notes: The A's story starts with a first-place record even though the run differential is still negative.
Springs and Ginn give the show a pitching-preview hook.
Use the Denzel Clarke and Colby Thomas move only as a verified roster note.

rockies.headline: The Rockies conversation starts with how ugly the early math looks and whether there is any believable next-step hope.
verified_notes: Snapshot verified from MLB.com team pages, ESPN transactions, and Baseball Savant.
MLB.com and Baseball Savant align on the Rockies at 11-16 with a -16 run differential.
MLB.com's probable-pitchers page lists Jose Quintana for April 28 against Cincinnati.
MLB.com transactions page shows Ryan Feltner to the 15-day injured list and Sammy Peralta recalled on April 24.
notes: The Rockies tone starts with a bad run differential and a rough early record.
Quintana gives you a preview peg.
The Feltner and Peralta move is a verified transaction note for the pitching conversation.

tigers.headline: The Tigers feel like the most upward-looking homer segment because the record is winning and the run differential says it might not be fake.
verified_notes: Snapshot verified from MLB.com team pages, ESPN transactions, and Baseball Savant.
Baseball Savant lists the Tigers at 14-13, second in the AL Central, with a +12 run differential.
MLB.com has a Tigers probable-pitchers page for the club's upcoming starts.
MLB.com shows Bailey Horn to the 60-day injured list and Burch Smith selected on April 22.
ESPN shows Parker Meadows transferred from the 10-day injured list to the 60-day injured list on April 13.
notes: The Tigers segment should sound more hopeful because the team has a winning record and positive run differential.
Use the AL Central position and run differential to give the segment structure.
Keep injury and roster notes strictly tied to MLB.com and ESPN logs.
